contextBlurb: The United States federal landscape is marked by an unusually high volume of AI and data center legislation concentrated almost entirely in committee, with no bills yet enacted — signaling active concern but legislative gridlock. Distinctive pressure points include multiple competing bills targeting data center energy costs and grid strain on ratepayers (SB3852, HB8033, SB3682), alongside a cluster of AI governance proposals spanning algorithmic transparency, child safety, civil rights, and workforce impacts. The breadth of filings reflects deep federal ambivalence: lawmakers are clearly alarmed across party lines, but consensus on any single framework remains elusive.
